meta.title: HLX Unified Codex
meta.architecture_statement: HLX specifies canonical encoding, ordering, and transfer of values. It does not define semantic meaning or execution behavior.
meta.purpose: Didactic injection of the HLX Language Family into LLM context. This file is the single source of truth for bootstrapping HLX capability.

1_axioms.description: The inviolable truths governing the Helix architecture.

axioms.name: DETERMINISM
axioms.rule: The same input code must ALWAYS produce the same Latent Collapse (LC) stream.

axioms.name: REVERSIBILITY
axioms.rule: Any value compressed into a Handle (⚳) can be resolved (⚯) back to its exact original state.

axioms.name: BIJECTION
axioms.rule: Track A (Lite/ASCII) and Track B (Runic/Glyph) are mathematically isomorphic. They map 1:1.

axioms.name: UNIVERSAL_VALUE
axioms.rule: All tracks lower to the HLX-Lite Value System (Contracts 1-5) before encoding.

contract_semantics_boundary.statement:
HLX CONTRACT SEMANTICS BOUNDARY:

HLX defines the canonical structure, encoding, ordering, and transport of values.

Contracts 1–5 define the HLX-Lite value mechanics:
- Primitive value representation
- Object framing (contract_id + sorted field indices)
- Deterministic encoding rules

Contract IDs above the primitive set (e.g., 14) represent user-defined schemas.

HLX does not define the semantic meaning of user-defined contracts.
Their interpretation is external to the language and enforced by runtimes or applications.

The LLM MUST treat unknown contract IDs as opaque but structurally valid if they conform to encoding rules.

2_architecture.description: The Dual-Track Architecture. Two surface languages, one runtime.

track_a_lite.name: HLX-Lite (Engineering)
track_a_lite.pipeline: HLXL (Source) -> HLXL-LS (Latent Ops) -> HLX-Lite Value -> LC Stream

track_b_runic.name: HLX-Runic (Native)
track_b_runic.pipeline: HLX (Source) -> HLX-LS (Latent Ops) -> HLX-Lite Value -> LC Stream

3_foundation_values.description: The atomic type system underlying all HLX languages. It defines HOW data is structured.

field_notation.description: @N denotes zero-based field index N within a contract object.
field_notation.rule: Fields MUST be encoded in ascending index order in LC-B. Violations raise E_FIELD_ORDER.

4_transliteration.description: Mandatory ASCII transliteration table for all canonical glyphs.

5_latent_collapse.name: LC (Latent Collapse)
5_latent_collapse.description: The universal wire format. Defined in two modes as per SD9.

lc_t_text.description: Human-readable projection for LLMs and debugging.

lc_b_binary.description: Authoritative binary form for hashing and storage (v2).
lc_b_binary.object_rule: Fields encoded as <field_idx_leb128><VALUE>. Must be sorted by index.

lc_12_envelope_spec.merkle_spec: MERKLE SPEC (FANOUT 16, CANONICAL): Let chunks be ordered by index i = 0..N-1. Leaf hash: H_leaf[i] = BLAKE3(chunk_bytes[i]). Group leaves in order into nodes of up to 16 children. INTERNAL NODE (CANONICAL): - Let child_count be the number of children in this node, where 1 ≤ child_count ≤ 16. - Let child_hashes be the ordered list of child hashes for this node. - Compute the internal node hash as: H_node = BLAKE3( byte(child_count) || concat(child_hash_0 || child_hash_1 || ... || child_hash_(child_count-1)) ) - child_hash_i MUST be the 32-byte BLAKE3 digest of the corresponding child. - Children MUST be concatenated in strictly increasing child index order. - No padding hashes are used. - No implicit normalization of fanout is permitted. VERIFICATION RULE: - A receiver MUST recompute leaf hashes, rebuild the tree using this exact rule, and compare the resulting payload_root to the manifest. - Any mismatch is fatal (E_ENV_PAYLOAD_HASH_MISMATCH).
lc_12_envelope_spec.table_order_key: TABLE ORDER KEY (CANONICAL): Normalize handle string with Unicode NFC. Lowercase using Unicode simple case-folding. Strip exactly one leading literal "&h_" prefix if present; strip nothing else. order_key = UTF-8 bytes of resulting string. Sort ascending lexicographic by order_key bytes. - Case folding MUST use Unicode 15.0 simple case folding as defined in Unicode Character Database file CaseFolding.txt.
